feature: URL-based Social Media Connections - Users can paste profile URLs instead of API keys to connect platforms
summary: Tested new URL-based connection feature for Social Media Dashboard. All 30 backend API tests pass (100%). All frontend UI elements verified including URL/API mode toggle in credential modal, platform badges (API, URL, via URL), live count badge in Analytics tab.

backend_api_tests.test: GET /api/social/url-supported includes expected platforms (youtube, twitter, reddit, tiktok, etc)
POST /api/social/connect-url connects TikTok using profile URL
POST /api/social/connect-url auto-detects platform when not provided
POST /api/social/connect-url saves credentials with profile_url field
POST /api/social/connect-url/bulk connects multiple URLs at once
GET /api/social/connections includes has_url_connect, connection_method, profile_url, url_example
GET /api/social/connections URL-connected platforms have connection_method='url' and profile_url
GET /api/social/metrics/dashboard shows source_method='url' for URL connections
Dashboard live_count includes URL-connected platforms with real data

frontend_tests.test: Credential Modal: Shows 'Profile URL' / 'API Keys' tab toggle when platform has URL support
Credential Modal: URL mode shows profile URL input with platform-specific placeholder/example
Credential Modal: Quick Connect info box explains 'No API keys needed'
Credential Modal: 'Connect with URL' button shown in URL mode
Platform Cards: show 'API' badge (cyan) for platforms with live API support
Platform Cards: show 'URL' badge (purple) for URL-only platforms like Pinterest
Platform Cards: show 'via URL' badge for platforms connected via URL method
Analytics tab: shows '1 Live' badge reflecting URL-connected platforms with real data
Analytics tab: shows '118 Simulated' badge for simulated platforms
Analytics tab: Live API Integration Active info banner present

url_supported_endpoint.description: Returns 13 platforms with URL adapters and url_examples dict

url_detect_endpoint.description: Auto-detects platform from URL, returns platform_id, username, has_live_metrics, url_example

connect_url_endpoint.description: Connects platform using profile URL, saves credentials with profile_url field, returns initial_metrics when available

bulk_connect_url_endpoint.description: Bulk connect multiple URLs at once, handles invalid URLs gracefully

connections_url_fields.description: Each connection now includes URL-related metadata

dashboard_source_method.description: Platforms show source_method='url' when connected via URL and returning live data

frontend_credential_modal.description: Modal shows URL/API mode toggle when platform.has_url_connect=true AND has credentials_required
url_mode_features: Profile URL input with platform-specific placeholder

frontend_platform_badges.api_badge: Cyan 'API' badge for platforms with live API adapters (9 platforms)
frontend_platform_badges.via_url_badge: Green 'via URL' badge for platforms connected via URL method

frontend_analytics_badges.description: Analytics tab shows live count badge reflecting URL-connected platforms with real data

critical_code_review_comments: 13 URL adapters properly implemented for major platforms
URL detection patterns handle multiple URL formats per platform (e.g., youtube.com/@, /channel/, /user/, /c/)
connect-url endpoint properly saves profile_url in credentials and sets connection_method='url'
source_method field added to dashboard metrics to distinguish API vs URL data sources
Frontend modal properly shows URL/API toggle only when platform supports both modes
All data-testid attributes present for automated testing
seed_data_creation: None - platforms connected via URL during testing: TikTok (@khaby.lame), YouTube (@MrBeast), Reddit (testuser123)

mocked_api.description: URL-based fetchers attempt to scrape real public pages/APIs. TikTok URL fetching returns real data (160.3M followers for @khaby.lame). Other platforms may fall back to simulated if scraping fails.

context_for_next_testing_agent: URL-based connection feature complete. Key endpoints: GET /api/social/url-supported (13 platforms), POST /api/social/url-detect (auto-detect platform from URL), POST /api/social/connect-url (connect via URL), POST /api/social/connect-url/bulk (bulk connect). Connections have has_url_connect, connection_method, profile_url, url_example fields. Dashboard shows source_method='url' for URL-connected platforms with live data. Frontend credential modal has URL/API toggle when platform supports both. Platform cards show API/URL/via URL badges. Analytics tab shows live count badge.
